description: Use for the cheapest, fastest broad pass when you want one general-purpose analyst rather than specialist coverage.
Best for light triage, simple questions, or when latency matters more than depth.
Use for normal static-analysis requests that benefit from a few core specialists without a large swarm.
Best default when you want Ghidra, control-flow, strings, and obfuscation coverage with moderate cost.
Use for broader static-analysis coverage when capability mapping should be included in addition to the core specialists.
Best for normal multi-angle investigation where no special editing or reconstruction focus is needed.
Use when runtime-behavior interpretation matters and safe dynamic tooling is available or relevant.
Best for cases where static reasoning should be complemented by a runtime-focused specialist.
Use for deeper static-analysis coverage when you want more parallel control-flow attention and higher breadth.
Best for tougher binaries where extra static specialist overlap is worth the additional cost and contention risk.
Use when the work is heavily Ghidra-centric and depends on xrefs, function relationships, and type recovery more than broad capability triage.
Best for control-flow reconstruction, function relevance decisions, and deeper static reversing inside Ghidra.
Use when the goal is to explain or reconstruct functionality in C/C++ terms rather than just triage malware behavior.
Best for example implementations, pseudocode cleanup, type recovery, and source-like reconstruction.
Use when the user wants rename, retype, comment, refactor, or approval-ready Ghidra edit proposals.
Best for identifying a real main/dispatcher, improving function or variable names, refining prototypes, and preparing change-queue proposals for approval.
